mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below the 6.5 percent threshold signals labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate profit expectations, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered observations, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when unemployment crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly lifts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day horizons, or forward returns are not significantly positive when both series trend upward simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansion phases reflects peak manufacturing capacity utilization, which compresses profit margins and generates negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases, or the negative correlation breaks down (Rank IC > 0.0) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the consumer price index above 238.0 triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs and forces equity valuation compression.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of threshold-crossing events, or the inflation threshold shows zero predictive accuracy (Rank IC = 0).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market expansion, which sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term produces a negative predictive coefficient, or forward returns are negative in over 55% of cases when both labor indicators move in the specified direction.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum, which directly supports corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero when GDP growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sudden supply-side contraction, which triggers recession fears and forces immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered observations, or the threshold condition fails to produce a statistically significant negative return (p > 0.05).
